import pandas as pd
import ast

# Load literature & fiction books data
books_data = var_functions.query_db:20
books_df = pd.DataFrame(books_data)

print(f"Loaded {len(books_df)} Literature & Fiction books")

# Load reviews data (file path)
reviews_file = var_functions.query_db:21
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews_data = json.load(f)
reviews_df = pd.DataFrame(reviews_data)

print(f"Loaded {len(reviews_df)} reviews")

# Convert rating to numeric
reviews_df['rating'] = pd.to_numeric(reviews_df['rating'], errors='coerce')

# Calculate average rating per book
avg_ratings = reviews_df.groupby('purchase_id')['rating'].agg(['mean', 'count']).reset_index()
avg_ratings.columns = ['purchase_id', 'avg_rating', 'review_count']

# Filter for perfect 5.0 average rating
perfect_ratings = avg_ratings[avg_ratings['avg_rating'] == 5.0]
print(f"Found {len(perfect_ratings)} books with perfect 5.0 rating")

# Merge with Literature & Fiction books
# book_id in books matches purchase_id in reviews
merged = books_df.merge(
    perfect_ratings,
    left_on='book_id',
    right_on='purchase_id',
    how='inner'
)

print(f"Found {len(merged)} Literature & Fiction books with perfect 5.0 rating")

if len(merged) > 0:
    result = merged[['title', 'book_id', 'rating_number', 'categories', 'avg_rating', 'review_count']].copy()
    result_dict = result.to_dict('records')
    
    print("\nMatching books:")
    for book in result_dict:
        print(f"- {book['title']} (Book ID: {book['book_id']}, Reviews: {book['review_count']})")
else:
    result_dict = []

result_json = json.dumps(result_dict, default=str)
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result_json)"}

reviews_df['rating'] = pd.to_numeric(reviews_df['rating'], errors='coerce')

# Calculate average rating for each book (purchase_id)
avg_ratings = reviews_df.groupby('purchase_id')['rating'].agg(['mean', 'count']).reset_index()
avg_ratings.columns = ['purchase_id', 'avg_rating', 'review_count']

print(f"Average ratings calculated for {len(avg_ratings)} books")

# Find books with perfect 5.0 average rating
perfect_ratings = avg_ratings[avg_ratings['avg_rating'] == 5.0]
print(f"Books with perfect 5.0 rating: {len(perfect_ratings)}")

# Match with Literature & Fiction books
# book_id in books_df should match purchase_id in reviews_df
merged = books_df.merge(
    perfect_ratings,
    left_on='book_id',
    right_on='purchase_id',
    how='inner'
)

print(f"Literature & Fiction books with 5.0 rating: {len(merged)}")

if len(merged) > 0:
    # Sort by review count (descending) to show most reviewed first
    merged = merged.sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)
    
    result = merged[['title', 'book_id', 'rating_number', 'categories', 'avg_rating', 'review_count']]
    print("\nMatching books:")
    for _, book in result.iterrows():
        print(f"- {book['title']}")
        print(f"  Book ID: {book['book_id']}")
        print(f"  Reviews: {book['review_count']}")
        print(f"  Rating: {book['avg_rating']}")
        print(f"  Category: {book['categories']}")
        print()
    
    result_dict = result.to_dict('records')
else:
    # Debug: Check if any Literature & Fiction books have reviews at all
    all_merged = books_df.merge(
        avg_ratings,
        left_on='book_id',
        right_on='purchase_id',
        how='inner'
    )
    print(f"\nTotal Literature & Fiction books with reviews: {len(all_merged)}")
    
    if len(all_merged) > 0:
        # Show rating distribution
        rating_counts = all_merged['avg_rating'].value_counts().sort_index()
        print("Rating distribution:")
        for rating, count in rating_counts.items():
            print(f"  {rating}: {count} books")
    
    result_dict = []

result_json = json.dumps(result_dict, default=str)
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result_json)"}

{"code": "import json
import pandas as pd

# Load Literature & Fiction books data
books_data = var_functions.query_db_50
books_df = pd.DataFrame(books_data)

# Load reviews data from file
reviews_file = var_functions.query_db_51
with open(reviews_file, 'r') as f:
    reviews_data = json.load(f)
reviews_df = pd.DataFrame(reviews_data)

# Convert rating to numeric
reviews_df['rating'] = pd.to_numeric(reviews_df['rating'], errors='coerce')

# Calculate average rating for each book (purchase_id)
avg_ratings = reviews_df.groupby('purchase_id')['rating'].agg(['mean', 'count']).reset_index()
avg_ratings.columns = ['purchase_id', 'avg_rating', 'review_count']

# Filter for perfect 5.0 average rating
perfect_ratings = avg_ratings[avg_ratings['avg_rating'] == 5.0]

# Merge with Literature & Fiction books (book_id matches purchase_id)
merged = books_df.merge(
    perfect_ratings,
    left_on='book_id',
    right_on='purchase_id',
    how='inner'
)

# Prepare final result
if len(merged) > 0:
    result = merged[['title', 'book_id', 'rating_number', 'categories', 'avg_rating', 'review_count']]
    result_sorted = result.sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)
    result_dict = result_sorted.to_dict('records')
else:
    result_dict = []

# Output as JSON string
result_json = json.dumps(result_dict, default=str)
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result_json)"}

import pandas as pd

# Load Literature & Fiction books
books_data = var_functions.query_db_50

# Load pre-calculated perfect 5.0 ratings

perfect_ratings_df = pd.DataFrame(perfect_ratings_data)
print(f"Found {len(perfect_ratings_df)} books with perfect 5.0 rating")

# Merge the dataframes on book_id (books) and purchase_id (reviews)
merged = books_df.merge(
    perfect_ratings_df,
    left_on='book_id',
    right_on='purchase_id',
    how='inner'
)

print(f"Found {len(merged)} matches")

if len(merged) > 0:
    # Sort by review count (descending) to show most reviewed books first
    result = merged.sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)
    
    # Display the results
    print("\nMatching books:")
    for _, book in result.iterrows():
        print(f"Title: {book['title']}")
        print(f"Book ID: {book['book_id']}")
        print(f"Average Rating: {book['avg_rating']}")
        print(f"Number of Reviews: {book['review_count']}")
        print(f"Categories: {book['categories']}")
        print(f"Total Ratings: {book['rating_number']}")
        print("-" * 50)
    
    # Prepare final result as list of dictionaries
    final_result = result[['title', 'book_id', 'rating_number', 'categories', 'avg_rating', 'review_count']].to_dict('records')
else:
    final_result = []

# Output as JSON string
result_json = json.dumps(final_result, default=str)
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result_json)"}

{"code": "import json
import pandas as pd

# Load Literature & Fiction books
books_data = var_functions.query_db_66
books_df = pd.DataFrame(books_data)

# Load all books with perfect 5.0 ratings
perfect_ratings_data = var_functions.query_db_80
perfect_ratings_df = pd.DataFrame(perfect_ratings_data)

# Convert data types
perfect_ratings_df['avg_rating'] = perfect_ratings_df['avg_rating'].astype(float)
perfect_ratings_df['review_count'] = perfect_ratings_df['review_count'].astype(int)

# Merge on book_id (books) and purchase_id (reviews)
merged_df = books_df.merge(
    perfect_ratings_df,
    left_on='book_id',
    right_on='purchase_id',
    how='inner'
)

# Prepare final results sorted by review count
results = []
if len(merged_df) > 0:
    merged_df = merged_df.sort_values('review_count', ascending=False)
    for _, row in merged_df.iterrows():
        results.append({
            'title': row['title'],
            'book_id': row['book_id'],
            'avg_rating': row['avg_rating'],
            'review_count': row['review_count'],
            'categories': row['categories'],
            'rating_number': row['rating_number']
        })

# Return results as JSON
result_json = json.dumps(results, default=str)
print('__RESULT__:')
print(result_json)"}

var_functions.query_db:9.title: Ha! On me!  I thought this was a cookbook!
var_functions.query_db:9.text:
Lucky for me it's more than a cookbook.

The story of a modern Daniel Boone, growing up in Michigan, one of three sons of a hunter. Beginning at age eight when he shot a squirrel, to age 13 his first deer, then hunting his way through college, a career as a fur trapper, hunting sheep in Alaska, canoeing the Missouri River for deer and ending in Brooklyn age 37. Brooklyn?

I especially liked that I share his disgust at catch & release fishing - if you fish it, you have to eat it - anything else is sadism (my words).

My brother in law is the cook in the family and I wanted to see if this would be a good gift for him and I struck pay dirt following each chapter are "Tasting Notes" where the author speaks of cooking wild game.

I also enjoyed it for the history of the land he has hunted as well as the history of the hunt (I too was a big Daniel Boone fan growing up!).

I think more pictures (everything but dead things) would have made this a keeper.

var_functions.query_db:9.title: A wonderful adventure in France
var_functions.query_db:9.text:
I loved this book all the way until the end. I have recently discovered that the author is intending to release another book, and from what I understand it will continue where this one left off. I am thankful for this, as the way this book ended was definitely a negative for me. I wanted to know more! What happened to the author, did she learn what she came to learn? Did she get the job she was hoping for? So many questions.

From the beginning of Linda's book, it is easy to love her. She is open, honest and definitely has the type of personality you want your heroine to have, whether the book is fiction or reality. I couldn't help but root for Linda throughout her trials with her host family, even when I thought she acted as a bit of a brat herself. Listening to her internal thoughts about what she went through and her desire to achieve her goals made this book feel close to my heart.

I applaud the adventurous spirit of the author and her decision to keep journal entries from that chapter of her life. What great material to have later to inspire a book! While I enjoyed the entire memoir, my favourite part of this book would have to be the author's descriptions of the many places she visited and the people she met along the way. While I think she could definitely have made a better impression on the family she worked for if she had been honest about her lack of French language skills from the beginning, she is a pioneer. Her drive and desire to learn the language from those in the actual country was inspiring. Not many people would have been gutsy enough to do what Linda did.

I also particularly enjoyed the relationship between Linda and Antoine. The Kind heart of the author was apparent in her actions toward the children, even when she struggled with them.

There is some romance, and I appreciated the way the author handled it. While sex scenes are not necessarily automatically offensive, there is something about memoirs that makes me uncomfortable if they are filled with them. This author manages to hint at her experiences without going into too much detail, leaving the reader to decide exactly what happened. Great writing.

When you step into reading this book, you learn a lot about French customs, French cuisine and wine. You also get the opportunity to brush up on your French skills a bit and get to step outside yourself and live an adventure through the eyes of the author. I read this book in one sitting with very few breaks in between and found myself enthralled in the sights, sounds and atmosphere of this memoir.

In the end, I was disappointed that the book ended. I could have kept reading about Linda's experiences without stopping anytime in the foreseeable future. The end of the book leaves many unanswered questions, that I am assuming the author will be answering in her next book. Were this a standalone, I would be very disappointed with the way the book ended, but with the knowledge that she is writing another book to continue with her story, I can't fault this one. One thing is for sure, if you read this book, you will be anxiously awaiting the next.

I would recommend this to anyone who likes books about travel, memoirs or just a good story that came from the recollections and heart of the author. Read it, you will be glad you did. Overall, this was a VERY enjoyable read that gave me many reasons to wish I could drop everything and head off to France!
